name: AI Cold Email Drafter for Medical & Dental Practices

parameters.content:
## AI Cold Email Drafter for Medical & Dental Practices

### How it works

This workflow manually processes a Google Sheets lead list for medical and dental practices, selecting only pending records and handling them one at a time. For each lead, it scrapes the practice website with Apify, prepares and validates the scraped text, then uses OpenAI to analyze the practice and draft a tailored cold email. Successful drafts are saved back to Google Sheets with a rate-limit pause between leads, while failed scrapes are marked and skipped.

### Setup steps

- Configure Google Sheets credentials and select the spreadsheet, sheet, and columns used for lead input, draft output, pending status, and scrape-failure status.
- Configure Apify credentials and set the actor plus input fields needed to scrape each lead’s website URL.
- Configure OpenAI credentials and review the prompts/model settings for both the analysis step and the final email drafting step.
- Verify the filter conditions for pending leads and the code-node field mappings so they match the exact column names in your sheet.
- Set an appropriate wait duration in the rate-limit node for your Apify, OpenAI, and Google Sheets usage limits.

### Customization

Adjust the pending-status filter, scrape actor settings, OpenAI prompts, A/B framing logic, and output columns to match your target specialty, email style, and sales process.

## Load pending leads

Starts the workflow manually, reads independent practice leads from Google Sheets, filters to pending rows, and feeds them into a batch loop for one-at-a-time processing.

## Scrape practice website

Runs an Apify actor for the current lead, retrieves the actor dataset items, combines scraped pages into a trimmed text blob, and checks whether enough usable content was collected.

## Analyze scraped content

Sends validated scraped website text to an OpenAI model for analysis, then parses the model response and selects an A/B positioning or framing strategy for the outreach email.

## Handle scrape failure

Marks leads with insufficient or failed scraped content as failed in Google Sheets, then routes control back to the lead loop to continue with the next record.

## Generate email draft

Uses the selected framing and analysis context to have OpenAI draft the cold email, then formats the result into a row structure ready for storage.

## Save and throttle

Writes the completed draft back to Google Sheets and waits before returning to the batch loop, helping respect rate limits across scraping, AI, and sheet operations.

values.content:
=You are a web/conversion consultant who reviews US medical & dental practice websites for Jainam, a freelance developer who builds AI phone-call assistants, website chatbots, and appointment-booking automation for clinics.

Your job: read the scraped website text and find 2-3 CONCRETE, factual gaps where one of Jainam's services would genuinely help the practice get and serve more patients. Focus especially on:
- How patients currently book (phone-only? printable PDF form? no online booking?)
- Whether there is any chatbot / live chat / instant answer for visitor questions
- After-hours / missed-call handling
- New-patient intake (manual paper forms vs automated)
- Anything that creates phone-tag or front-desk load

Ground EVERY observation strictly in the provided text. Never invent features the text doesn't mention. If the site genuinely looks modern with online booking already, say so honestly and pick a softer angle.

Output STRICT JSON only — no markdown, no backticks:
{
  "practice_summary": "one sentence on what this practice does and who runs it",
  "observations": [
    {"area": "e.g. booking / chatbot / after-hours / intake", "finding": "specific factual observation from the text", "opportunity": "which of Jainam's services (AI call assistant, website chatbot, booking automation, intake automation) would help and the patient/staff benefit"}
  ],
  "top_angle": "the single strongest service to pitch — one of: AI phone-call assistant, website AI chatbot, online appointment booking, patient intake automation"
}
Rules: max 3 observations. Be specific to THIS practice, not generic.

=You write short, human cold outreach emails for Jainam Jain, a freelance developer who builds AI phone-call assistants, website chatbots, and appointment-booking automation specifically for medical and dental practices.

Portfolio: jainam.dev
Calendly: https://calendly.com/jainamjain742/ai-automation

Audience: US medical/dental practice owners (busy doctors). Goal: book a 15-minute call.

Rules:
- Under 130 words. Plain, warm, human — like a real person who actually looked at their website.
- Reference ONE concrete observation from the analysis (their actual booking/intake/after-hours gap).
- Pitch the single most relevant service (AI call assistant, chatbot, or online booking) in terms of patient/staff BENEFIT, never tech jargon. Never say "LangChain", "API", "n8n" etc.
- Never invent fake stats or fake client names.
- Always end with the Calendly link.
- Always end with this exact unsubscribe line as the final paragraph: "Not relevant? Reply 'unsubscribe' and I won't reach out again."

Output STRICT JSON only, no markdown, no backticks:
{"subject":"...","body":"..."}
The body must be plain text with line breaks written as \n.

=Recipient: Dr. {{ $json.LastName }} at {{ $json.Domain }}
Framing style: {{ $json.framing }}  (soft = frame the gap gently as an "opportunity I noticed", warm and respectful; direct = name the gap more plainly but still polite — never insulting)

Analysis JSON:
{{ $json.analysisJson }}

Write the email using top_angle and the single most relevant observation. Make it specific to THIS practice, not generic.

Sign exactly as:
Jainam Jain
AI Automation Developer
jainam.dev
